req.title: Reset employee password
req.statement: An employee shall reset their password via an email link that expires after 30 minutes; expired links must be rejected.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers four distinct testable behaviours: (1) successful reset with a valid, unexpired token (EP positive path); (2) rejection of an expired token that is clearly past the 30-minute window (NEG/DT); (3) the exact 30-minute expiry boundary – a token used at t=30 min must be rejected while one used just before must succeed (BVA); (4) state transition confirming all existing sessions are invalidated after a successful reset (STC); and (5) password-policy violation returning 422 (DT negative). These five cases satisfy the minimum three-case requirement while providing full decision-table and state coverage of the requirement's error pathways and expected outcomes. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up their own tokens/sessions.

tests.name: test_reset_password_with_valid_token_succeeds
preconditions: A valid, unexpired reset token exists for an active employee account
The new password satisfies the system password policy
actions: POST /password/reset with the valid token and a policy-compliant new password
Attempt login with the new password
Assert HTTP 200 on reset and successful login
expected: Employee can authenticate with the new password
Old password is no longer accepted
trace: R-WEB-102: trigger – employee submits new password
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_reset_password_with_valid_token_succeeds(
    api_client,           # <fixture> base-URL-aware requests.Session
    valid_reset_token,    # <fixture> yields (token, employee_creds) for a fresh account
    policy_password,      # <fixture> returns a password that satisfies policy
):
    token, creds = valid_reset_token
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/password/reset",
        json={"token": token, "new_password": policy_password},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"

    login_resp = api_client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        json={"username": creds["username"], "password": policy_password},
    )
    assert login_resp.status_code == 200, "Login with new password should succeed"

    old_login = api_client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        json={"username": creds["username"], "password": creds["old_password"]},
    )
    assert old_login.status_code in (401, 403), "Old password must be rejected"

tests.name: test_reset_password_with_expired_token_returns_410
preconditions: A reset token that was issued more than 30 minutes ago exists
The token has not been used previously
actions: POST /password/reset with the expired token and a policy-compliant new password
Assert employee password has NOT changed
expected: Response body contains an error indicating token expiry
trace: R-WEB-102: error pathway – 410 when token expired
R-WEB-102: email link expires after 30 minutes
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_reset_password_with_expired_token_returns_410(
    api_client,              # <fixture>
    expired_reset_token,     # <fixture> yields (token, employee_creds) aged > 30 min
    policy_password,         # <fixture>
):
    token, creds = expired_reset_token
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/password/reset",
        json={"token": token, "new_password": policy_password},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 410, f"Expected 410, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"

    body = resp.json()
    assert "expired" in body.get("error", "").lower() or \
           "expired" in body.get("message", "").lower(), \
           "Response must indicate token expiry"

    login_resp = api_client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        json={"username": creds["username"], "password": creds["old_password"]},
    )
    assert login_resp.status_code == 200, "Password must remain unchanged after expired-token attempt"

tests.name: test_reset_token_expires_exactly_at_30_minutes
preconditions: Two reset tokens exist: one aged 29 min 59 sec (within window) and one aged 30 min 00 sec (at boundary)
actions: POST /password/reset with the token aged 29 min 59 sec – assert 200
POST /password/reset with the token aged exactly 30 min 00 sec – assert 410
expected: Token at 29 min 59 sec: HTTP 200 – password updated
Token at 30 min 00 sec: HTTP 410 – token rejected as expired
trace: R-WEB-102: email link expires after 30 minutes
R-WEB-102: boundary – last valid second vs first invalid second
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_reset_token_expires_exactly_at_30_minutes(
    api_client,                  # <fixture>
    near_expiry_reset_token,     # <fixture> yields token issued 29 min 59 sec ago
    at_expiry_reset_token,       # <fixture> yields token issued exactly 30 min ago
    policy_password,             # <fixture>
    alt_policy_password,         # <fixture> second distinct compliant password
):
    # --- within window (29:59) must succeed ---
    resp_valid = api_client.post(
        "/password/reset",
        json={"token": near_expiry_reset_token, "new_password": policy_password},
    )
    assert resp_valid.status_code == 200, (
        f"Token at 29:59 should be accepted; got {resp_valid.status_code}"
    )

    # --- at boundary (30:00) must be rejected ---
    resp_expired = api_client.post(
        "/password/reset",
        json={"token": at_expiry_reset_token, "new_password": alt_policy_password},
    )
    assert resp_expired.status_code == 410, (
        f"Token at 30:00 should be rejected; got {resp_expired.status_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_successful_reset_invalidates_all_existing_sessions
preconditions: An active employee account has at least two live sessions (session cookies / JWT tokens)
A valid, unexpired reset token exists for that account
actions: Capture two active session tokens for the employee
POST /password/reset with a valid token and policy-compliant new password
Attempt an authenticated request using each of the two old session tokens
Assert all old sessions are rejected with 401
expected: All pre-existing session tokens return HTTP 401 on subsequent authenticated requests
trace: R-WEB-102: expected outcome – all sessions invalidated
R-WEB-102: state transition from authenticated → unauthenticated for pre-reset sessions

tests.name: test_reset_password_violating_policy_returns_422
preconditions: A valid, unexpired reset token exists for an active employee account
A password string that violates the system password policy is prepared
actions: POST /password/reset with the valid token and a policy-violating password
Assert the response body lists a policy violation reason
Assert the employee password has NOT changed
expected: Response body contains field-level validation error referencing password policy
Employee can still log in with the original password
trace: R-WEB-102: error pathway – 422 when password fails policy
R-WEB-102: trigger – employee submits new password (non-compliant)
tests.executable:
import pytest

@pytest.mark.parametrize("bad_password", [
    "short",           # too short
    "alllowercase1!",  # missing uppercase (example policy rule)
    "ALLUPPERCASE1!",  # missing lowercase
    "NoSpecialChar1",  # missing special character
])
def test_reset_password_violating_policy_returns_422(
    api_client,          # <fixture>
    valid_reset_token,   # <fixture> yields (token, employee_creds)
    bad_password,
):
    token, creds = valid_reset_token
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/password/reset",
        json={"token": token, "new_password": bad_password},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, (
        f"Expected 422 for password '{bad_password}', got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )

    body = resp.json()
    errors = body.get("errors") or body.get("detail") or []
    assert errors, "422 response must contain validation error details"

    login_resp = api_client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        json={"username": creds["username"], "password": creds["old_password"]},
    )
    assert login_resp.status_code == 200, "Password must remain unchanged after policy violation"
